actionable_feedback: {'id': 'af_01', 'title': 'Make spreadsheet machine-parseable or provide formula-export', 'description': "Provide a parseable spreadsheet or an environment-friendly alternate export so graders can verify formulas and row-level provenance. Options: include a plain-text CSV export of key calculation tables (with formulas shown as text in an adjacent 'Formulas' sheet), provide a second deliverable 'calculation_trace.csv' listing each derived cell, its formula, and its source input cell references, or include a PDF/CSV that explicitly shows formulas next to computed values.", 'priority': 'high'}

{'id': 'af_02', 'title': 'Add a clearly labeled decision table and explicit recommended action', 'description': "Add a dedicated 'Decision' section in the memo with a compact decision table that maps each threshold test to the recommended action (e.g., 'Activate discharge pull', 'Activate hallway protocol', 'Activate both', 'Consider diversion') and a single explicit recommended action (activate/no-activate/divert). Each decision-row should cite the worksheet name and cell range used to compute the inputs (row-level citations).", 'priority': 'high'}

{'id': 'af_03', 'title': 'Ensure memo includes full sensitivity outputs and is not truncated', 'description': 'Expand the memo to include the full sensitivity table(s) and threshold tests used in the workbook, and ensure the memo file is complete (no truncation); if memo length is an issue, attach supplemental files (e.g., sensitivity_summary.csv) and reference them from the memo.', 'priority': 'medium'}

{'id': 'af_04', 'title': 'Add reproducibility/traceability README', 'description': "Include a short README or worksheet named 'Traceability' that maps each reported metric in the memo to the workbook sheet and exact cell or named range, and documents assumptions and any rounding conventions used.", 'priority': 'medium'}

{'id': 'af_05', 'title': 'Provide environment-robust deliverable copies', 'description': 'Because openpyxl was unavailable in the grader environment, include alternate exports (CSV for key tables, formula-revealing CSV, or a zipped folder of worksheet XMLs) so automated graders can parse content irrespective of environment library availability.', 'priority': 'medium'}
artifact_specific_comments: {'artifact_ref': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comment': 'Workbook present but could not be parsed in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable). Provide alternate export or formula-revealing sheet.'}

{'artifact_ref': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comment': 'Memo contains inputs and many calculated results, but preview is truncated and no clearly labeled decision table or single recommended action is visible in the available preview.'}
feedback_summary: Required artifacts present and many intermediate calculations are visible in the memo preview, but the workbook could not be parsed in the evaluation environment and the memo preview is truncated. As a result workbook-level traceability (cell/formula provenance) and an explicit decision table / final recommended action could not be independently verified. Partial semantic credit (0.75) was assigned by the grader and verified with warnings.

verifier_issues_summary: Spreadsheet artifact could not be parsed so workbook-level formulas, per-cell provenance, and decision-table rows could not be independently verified.
Incident memo preview is truncated and does not show a clearly labeled decision table or explicit recommended action in the visible text.

limitations: Could not open/parse ed_boarding_surge.xlsx in this environment (openpyxl unavailable); semantic verification relies on memo preview only.
Memo preview is truncated; some expected sections (complete sensitivity table, explicit decision table and recommended action) are not fully visible in available preview.
metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model evaluator generated actionable feedback and revision plan.

actionable_feedback.description: Provide a parseable spreadsheet or an environment-friendly alternate export so graders can verify formulas and row-level provenance. Options: include a plain-text CSV export of key calculation tables (with formulas shown as text in an adjacent 'Formulas' sheet), provide a second deliverable 'calculation_trace.csv' listing each derived cell, its formula, and its source input cell references, or include a PDF/CSV that explicitly shows formulas next to computed values.
actionable_feedback.title: Make spreadsheet machine-parseable or provide formula-export

actionable_feedback.description: Add a dedicated 'Decision' section in the memo with a compact decision table that maps each threshold test to the recommended action (e.g., 'Activate discharge pull', 'Activate hallway protocol', 'Activate both', 'Consider diversion') and a single explicit recommended action (activate/no-activate/divert). Each decision-row should cite the worksheet name and cell range used to compute the inputs (row-level citations).
actionable_feedback.title: Add a clearly labeled decision table and explicit recommended action

actionable_feedback.description: Expand the memo to include the full sensitivity table(s) and threshold tests used in the workbook, and ensure the memo file is complete (no truncation); if memo length is an issue, attach supplemental files (e.g., sensitivity_summary.csv) and reference them from the memo.
actionable_feedback.title: Ensure memo includes full sensitivity outputs and is not truncated

actionable_feedback.description: Include a short README or worksheet named 'Traceability' that maps each reported metric in the memo to the workbook sheet and exact cell or named range, and documents assumptions and any rounding conventions used.
actionable_feedback.title: Add reproducibility/traceability README

actionable_feedback.description: Because openpyxl was unavailable in the grader environment, include alternate exports (CSV for key tables, formula-revealing CSV, or a zipped folder of worksheet XMLs) so automated graders can parse content irrespective of environment library availability.
actionable_feedback.title: Provide environment-robust deliverable copies

artifact_specific_comments.comment: Workbook present but could not be parsed in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable). Provide alternate export or formula-revealing sheet.
Memo contains inputs and many calculated results, but preview is truncated and no clearly labeled decision table or single recommended action is visible in the available preview.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce a revised submission that (1) includes an XLSX that is parseable in standard tooling or supplemental formula-revealing CSV(s), (2) expands the incident memo to include a labeled decision table with an explicit recommended action and row-level citations to the workbook cells/sheets that produced each key metric, (3) attaches the full sensitivity tables and a short Traceability README mapping memo entries to workbook locations, and (4) confirm the memo file is complete (no truncation).
trace_specific_comments: Trace shows the agent attempted dependency-free XLSX assembly after openpyxl and other spreadsheet libraries were unavailable; supply a parseable alternative to avoid environment dependency failures.

Steps and validation checks are present in the memo preview, which supports the grader's numeric checks, but independent verification of formulas and provenance requires parseable workbook exports or explicit formula lists.
